channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Why Ayodele Olawande’s NYSC reform is apt, timely and youthfully relevant
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Ayodele Olawande’s NYSC reform is a timely and relevant overhaul of the 53-year-old scheme. Discover the key changes and why they are crucial for youth dev
Read More: https://punchng.com/why-ayodele-olawandes-nysc-reform-is-apt-timely-and-youthfully-relevant/

item.title: What people born with one kidney should know – Nephrologists
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nephrologists explain how people born with one kidney can live healthy lives by adopting good habits and avoiding risks like obesity and certain medication
Read More: https://punchng.com/what-people-born-with-one-kidney-should-know-nephrologists/

item.title: inDrive appoints KieKie as Chief Wahala Officer
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
inDrive appoints media personality KieKie as Chief Wahala Officer to highlight Lagos transportation challenges and engage commuters in the #inDriveCWO camp
Read More: https://punchng.com/indrive-appoints-kiekie-as-chief-wahala-officer/

item.title: I’ve never created drama to stay relevant — Ugoccie
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigerian singer Ugoccie discusses her career, emphasizing talent and authenticity over controversy. Learn why she lets her music speak for itself.
Read More: https://punchng.com/ive-never-created-drama-to-stay-relevant-ugoccie/

item.title: DEAR 2026 gala celebrates leadership, excellence

item.title: Iyabo Aina bags NUJ honour

item.description:
Media practitioner Iyabo Aina receives a Certificate of Credence from the NUJ Lagos State Council for her contributions to the Correspondents’ Chapel.

item.description:
Academic City University, Ghana, leads higher education transformation in Africa, prioritising innovation, entrepreneurship, and hands-on learning.

item.title: Yikodeen partners Spanish firm
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Yikodeen Company Limited partners with Spain’s Sorbcontrol S.L to enhance oil spill response and industrial safety solutions in Nigeria’s key sectors.

item.title: Mbappe scores again to send France past stubborn Paraguay
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Kylian Mbappe's 70th-minute penalty gave France a 1-0 victory over Paraguay in an ill-tempered World Cup last-16 tie in Philadelphia to set up a quarter-final meeting with Morocco.

item.title: Primate Babatunde Ayodele: Wearing controversy like a cassock
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Liberians are still seething with rage weeks after Primate Elijah Babatunde Ayodele made a prophetic statement about their country. The post Primate Babatunde Ayodele: Wearing controversy like a cassock appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Toulouse thrash Catalans in all-French clash to dent Dragons' play-off hopes
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Toulouse Olympique claimed bragging rights in the French derby by scoring 10 tries in a 60-18 thrashing of Catalan Dragons.

item.description: Every successful digital product begins with an idea, but transforming that idea into a mobile or web application that people genuinely enjoy using is a challenge many startups fail to overcome. He has served as Secretary, Pan-Ndi Igbo Foundation, Ojo Local Government Area, former secretary and later chairman Ohanaeze Ndigbo in

item.title: Govt must treat creative industry as strategic national asset —Ekwuazi, ex-DG, NFC
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Few Nigerians have shaped the country’s film and broadcasting landscape as profoundly as Prof. Hyginus Ekwuazi. He is a distinguished film scholar, former Director-General of the Nigerian Film Corporation (NFC), and one of the true pioneers of film studies in Nigeria. He had also served as the pione

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The 2026 rainy season commenced with intensity nationwide some days ago, triggering widespread flooding across some parts of the country. item.title: Ticket prices plunge for USA-Belgium World Cup last-16 match
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The price for the final 2026 World ⁠Cup match in Seattle hit nearly $4,000 but dipped as low as $1,549 ⁠on Tuesday.

item.title: Alake warns mining firms over host community agreements, threatens licence revocation

item.description: “Host communities deserve to benefit from the resources in their land, and there will be consequences for those who ignore that responsibility.” The post Alake warns mining firms over host community agreements, threatens licence revocation appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Akiolu urges APC chairmen to shun money politics, predicts Tinubu’s re-election
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Akiolu disclosed that the APC in Edo State had set a target of delivering 2.5 million votes for President Tinubu in the next presidential election, attributing the optimism to what he described as the developmental strides of both the President and the Edo State Government.

item.title: Two more Nigerians killed in South Africa as Xenophobic violence escalates
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to the consulate, Musa Joe was allegedly killed by suspected criminals in Witbank, Mpumalanga Province, while Emeka Iroegbu reportedly died after being tortured by officers of the Tshwane Metro Police in Sunnyside, Pretoria.

item.title: Scotland make impressive start in statement away win over Argentina
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Scotland banished any lingering demons from November as they kicked off their Nations Championship with an impressive 47-38 away win over Argentina.

item.title: Ooni of Ife denies conferring chieftaincy title on Baba Ijesha
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Ooni of Ife, Oba Adeyeye Ogunwusi, has dismissed claims that he conferred a chieftaincy title on Nollywood actor and comedian Olanrewaju Omiyinka, popularly known as Baba Ijesha. The monarch said the reports circulating on social media were false and clarified that Baba Ijesha was not installed

item.title: Cooking gas price reduction update in Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The prices of liquefied petroleum gas, known as cooking gas, have continued to fall across parts of Nigeria after weeks of upsurge due to supply constraints.  Checks across cooking gas retail outlets in Abuja showed that they have dropped by between N1450 and N1500 per kilogram from N1700 at AA

item.description: Former Ogun State First Lady, Yeye Olufunke Daniel, has lost her mother, Chief Mrs. Sabainah Ibironke Opawole (née Osoba). The post Former Ogun first lady, Olufunke Daniel, loses 93-yr-old mother appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Kano Commences Distribution Of 90,000 Bags Of Fertiliser To Farmers
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: According to him, the consistent supply of subsidised inputs has led to higher crop yields, reduced food prices, a boost in rural employment and stronger food security in the state. The post Kano Commences Distribution Of 90,000 Bags Of Fertiliser To Farmers appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: South Africa: No Compensation for Nigerians who left property behind
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: South Africa has ruled out any compensation for Nigerians who left properties behind after being evacuated from the country. The country insisted that assets acquired illegally or located in informal settlements will not qualify for any form of reimbursement. The position was reaffirmed by South Afr

item.title: Osun guber: No one will go scot-free after committing a crime — IGP Disu
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Inspector-General of Police (IGP) Tunji Disu, has warned that anyone involved in political violence in Osun State will face the full weight of the law, declaring that “nobody will commit a crime and go scot-free.” Speaking in Osogbo on Friday, the police chief said he travelled from

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Brahim Diaz names team he wants Morocco to play in quarter-final
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Morocco playmaker, Brahim Diaz, has said he wants to face France in the quarter-final of the 2026 World Cup. Diaz provided two assists as the Atlas Lions beat co-hosts Canada 2-0 to qualify for the next round. The Real Madrid midfielder is now eager to come up against some of his club team-mates in

item.title: Northern Derby Formidable In Sustaining Culture, Tradition, Says Oluremi Tinubu
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The First Lady, Oluremi Tinubu has commended the organization of Northern Derbys, saying it was formidable in sustaining culture and tradition. She stated this at the 44th edition of the horse racing sports where she was represented by the wife of the Vice President Hajia Nana Shettima. According to

item.title: Gunmen kill female farmer, injure another in fresh Plateau attack
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Suspected armed bandits on Saturday attacked farmers at Rinyan village in Tahoss community of Riyom Local Government Area of Plateau State, killing one woman and injuring another. The post Gunmen kill female farmer, injure another in fresh Plateau attack appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Protocol Prying: Vulnerability Research in AirDrop and Quick Share
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26967 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48788849 Points: 8 # Comments: 1

item.title: Ex-Jigawa Governor Lamido criticises Pantami over past ‘infidel party’ remark on PDP
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Jigawa State Governor Sule Lamido has criticised former Minister of Communications and Digital Economy Isa Ali Pantami over his past description of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) as an “infidel party”, while reaffirming his long-standing loyalty to the opposition party. Lamido

item.title: NDC Deputy Chairman, scribe, others defect to PDP in Ebonyi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Ahead of the 2027 general elections, no fewer than 4,000 members of the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, including the Deputy Chairman, Mr. Okechukwu Idenyi, and the Secretary, Barr. Solomon Adum, have defected to the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) in Ebonyi State. The defectors were formally recei

item.title: Netanyahu ‘knows who the boss is’, says Trump
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Washington is Israel's closest ally, but Trump has been publicly critical of Netanyahu in recent weeks after Israel's war with Hezbollah in Lebanon threatened peace talks with Iran. The post Netanyahu ‘knows who the boss is’, says Trump appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Archaeologists uncover ancient Byzantine city in Egypt’s western desert
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: Well-preserved fourth-century quarters reveal details of daily life, urban development and economic activitiesArchaeologists in Egypt have uncovered a well-preserved Byzantine-era city in the western desert.The fourth-century quarters had residential and religious structures, including a basilica-st
